leg: goal 062 leg B — test-designer fixture capture + independent expected outputs
purpose: Re-verify the seven docs/regimes/canada_ciec.md §7 fixture pins against a fresh polite fetch (cross-session), and confirm the archived evidence bytes are byte-identical to the pinned shas. Closes the SAF §7 open question 'Cross-session byte stability of details pages' (spec leg had within-session 2/2 only; this leg is the cross-session confirmation, us_senate leg-B precedent).

client.tool: curl (Git Bash, Windows) — plain HTTPS GET, no browser, no cookies/session (source sets none, E44)
client.rate_limit: curl --rate 20/m on the 6-URL batch = one transfer per 3.0 s (>= the SAF's 2.2 s floor, invariant 10); the connectivity probe was a single standalone GET
client.conditional_get: not possible — the source serves no ETag/Last-Modified and sets no cookies (SAF §2.1, E44); the date window is the only incremental primitive

result.note: 7/7 raw-byte shas match the SAF §7 pins AND the docs/regimes/canada_ciec/evidence/{sha}.html archives (spec-leg E22/E23/E24/E25/E27/E28/E21). Raw-byte pinning held cross-session — no fallback to the normalized-content-hash was required. The fixture input.html files under crates/adapters/canada_ciec/fixtures/<case>/ are byte-identical copies of these archives (verified sha-equal at copy time).
